position: First element of the request line
explanation: Used to distinguish the operation type of HTTP requests, such as retrieving data, submitting data, modifying or deleting resources, and more.

position: Second element of the request line
explanation: Specifies the target resource upon which the request is intended to act. Its format varies depending on the method used.

position: Host header field in the request headers section
explanation: Indicates the domain name of the server and the port number on which the server is listening. Essential for HTTP/1.1 requests as it allows multiple domains to be served on the same IP address.

position: Max-Forwards header field in the request headers section
explanation: Defines a limit for the number of times a request can be forwarded by proxies, applicable mainly for TRACE and OPTIONS methods.

position: Upgrade header field in the request headers section
explanation: Used to request that the server switch to one of the listed protocols, allowing transition from HTTP/1.1 to other protocols.
